key_risks: A hot PCE, payrolls, or hawkish FOMC communication could lift yields and trigger a sharp growth-stock and semiconductor de-rating.
AI and semiconductor momentum may reverse quickly after extreme trailing gains, especially in Korea and Taiwan.
Geopolitical tension around Taiwan or global trade restrictions could disproportionately hurt semiconductor supply-chain exposures.
Regional banks could underperform if credit-quality, commercial real estate, or deposit-cost concerns resurface.
A broad risk-off move or volatility spike from crowded positioning could cause high-beta holdings to lag the S&P 500.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest AI-linked equity momentum in the universe, with 8.3% 30-day and very large 6-month/1-year gains; likely to keep attracting performance-chasing flows if risk appetite holds.
Exceptional recent country momentum and high semiconductor/export sensitivity provide leveraged exposure to the same AI hardware cycle.
Semiconductor-heavy market with strong 30-day and 6-month returns, offering additional AI supply-chain exposure with country diversification.
Broad US momentum factor has strong short- and medium-term trend persistence and can capture leadership outside a single sector.
Recent 30-day rebound and improving domestic cyclical tone may benefit from a Fed hold, resilient labor data, and a positively sloped yield curve.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio emphasizes the clearest one-month price leadership: AI/semiconductor supply chains, high-momentum country exposures, and a smaller cyclical catch-up position. Macro data show resilient growth with the Fed very likely on hold, supporting risk appetite despite elevated inflation.
rationale_summary: A concentrated high-beta momentum allocation offers the best chance to outperform the S&P 500 over the one-month window. Semiconductor, Korea, Taiwan, and US momentum strength are reinforced by recent price action, while regional banks add a domestic cyclical rebound component.
